subject: Chemistry
title: Cesium: The Atomic Clock Element
description: A fast breakdown of Cesium: The Atomic Clock Element for builders.

hook.text: One second is officially defined by cesium atoms vibrating exactly 9,192,631,770 times.

buildup.text: Cesium was discovered in 1860 by its sky-blue spectral lines. Its name means 'sky blue' in Latin.

discovery.text: Cesium atoms absorb microwaves at an incredibly precise frequency—perfect for timekeeping.

twist.text: Cesium clocks are so accurate they'd lose less than one second in 300 million years.

climax.text: GPS satellites rely on cesium clocks. Without them, your location would drift by miles.

punchline.text: A sky-blue element defines the second and guides GPS.

quiz.question: Why is cesium used in atomic clocks?
options: Its atoms absorb microwaves at an incredibly precise frequency
It is the heaviest stable element
It glows in the dark naturally
